label: MongoDB
description: Connect to a MongoDB database or Atlas cluster to query and manage documents.

form.title: Connect MongoDB
form.subtitle: Link Anton to a MongoDB database or Atlas cluster.

methods.label: Connection String
methods.description: Paste a full mongodb:// or mongodb+srv:// URI — the fastest way to connect. Includes credentials, host, and database in a single field.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with a Connection String

A MongoDB connection string encodes everything Anton needs in one value. This is the simplest path for both Atlas and self-hosted instances.

### MongoDB Atlas

1. Log in to [MongoDB Atlas](https://cloud.mongodb.com/).
2. Open your project and click **Database** in the left sidebar.
3. Click **Connect** next to the cluster you want.
4. Choose **Drivers** and select any driver — you just need the URI format.
5. Copy the string shown (it looks like `mongodb+srv://<user>:<password>@cluster0.abc.mongodb.net/`).
6. Replace `<password>` with your database user's password.
7. Append the database name after the host if not already present (e.g. `…mongodb.net/myDatabase`).
8. Paste the full string into the **Connection String** field below.

### Self-Hosted / On-Premises

1. Your string will typically look like `mongodb://username:password@host:27017/myDatabase`.
2. For replica sets, include the `?replicaSet=<name>` query parameter.
3. For TLS, append `?tls=true&tlsCAFile=/path/to/ca.pem` or equivalent.

### Notes

- Keep this string secret — it contains your credentials.
- Atlas IPs: add Anton's IP (or `0.0.0.0/0` for testing) under **Network Access → IP Access List**.
- If your password contains special characters, percent-encode them (e.g. `@` → `%40`).

fields.name: connection_string
fields.label: Connection String
fields.description: Full mongodb:// or mongodb+srv:// URI including credentials and database name.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Default Database
fields.description: Optional override for the default database. If your URI already contains a database name, leave this blank.

methods.label: Host + Credentials
methods.description: Specify the host, port, database, username, and password as separate fields. Useful when you don't have a pre-built connection string.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with Host and Credentials

Fill in each connection parameter individually. Anton assembles the connection string internally.

### Finding your connection details

**Atlas:**
1. Go to [MongoDB Atlas](https://cloud.mongodb.com/) → **Database → Connect → Drivers**.
2. Break apart the URI shown to extract host, username, and password.
3. The host looks like `cluster0.abc.mongodb.net` (no `mongodb+srv://` prefix needed here).

**Self-hosted:**
1. Your host is the server IP or hostname, and the port is typically `27017`.
2. Use the username/password for a database user with the appropriate roles.

### Notes

- Ensure the database user has at least `readWrite` on the target database.
- For Atlas, whitelist Anton's egress IP under **Network Access → IP Access List**.
- Enable **TLS** if your server requires encrypted connections (Atlas requires it by default).

fields.name: host
fields.label: Host
fields.description: Hostname or IP address of your MongoDB server or Atlas cluster (without protocol prefix).

fields.name: port
fields.label: Port
fields.description: MongoDB port. Defaults to 27017. Leave blank for Atlas SRV connections.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database
fields.description: The database name Anton will connect to by default.

fields.name: user
fields.label: Username
fields.description: MongoDB database user with appropriate read/write permissions.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Password for the database user. Stored securely.

fields.name: auth_source
fields.label: Auth Source Database
fields.description: The database where the user's credentials are stored. Typically 'admin' for self-hosted; may differ on Atlas.

fields.name: use_srv
fields.label: Use SRV (mongodb+srv://)
fields.description: Enable for Atlas or any cluster using DNS SRV records. Resolves port automatically.

fields.name: tls
fields.label: Enable TLS
fields.description: Encrypt the connection. Required for Atlas; optional for self-hosted servers with TLS configured.
